era: 1993-present. Hip-Hop/MC. E=5 -- the MC/artist side of Seattle's triple-threat hip-hop figure. Conception Records. Central District. RETURN from degree 138 (DJ/producer side). Dual-facet return pattern. Narrative rapping. Conversational delivery. Fourteenth degree in hip-hop era (134-147).

location.formative: Central District roots spanning three decades. The CD's hip-hop ecosystem is Vitamin D's creative home. Conception Records as CD hip-hop infrastructure.
location.career: Vitamin D. Triple threat: DJ, producer, MC. Solo albums, EPs, mixtapes, feature verses. Conception Records founder. Three decades in Seattle hip-hop.

members.name: Vitamin D
members.note: Triple threat. Central District. Narrative rapping. Conversational delivery. Geographically specific lyricism.

associated_musicians.name: Vitamin D (DJ/producer)
associated_musicians.note: DJ/producer at 138, MC/artist at 147. Dual-facet return. 9-degree gap.

associated_musicians.name: Sabzi
associated_musicians.note: Sabzi has 5 entries (99, 101, 122, 140, 145). Vitamin D has 2 entries (138, 147). Both document different facets.

associated_musicians.name: Seattle hip-hop scene
associated_musicians.note: Feature verses on dozens of tracks. Conception Records catalog. Central District anchor.

theory_nodes.concept: Dual-facet return pattern -- two entries for different facets of one artist; DJ/producer (138) vs. MC/artist (147); 9-degree gap; parallels Sabzi's multi-entry arc; engine treats multidimensionality as worth separate documentation
examples: 138: Vitamin D as DJ/producer. Beats, turntablism, studio.
147: Vitamin D as MC/artist. Rapping, lyricism, solo albums.
9-degree gap. Same person, different professional roles.
theory_nodes.description: Dual-facet return. Two entries for one multifaceted artist.

genealogy.relationship: Vitamin D MC at 147. Dual-facet return from 138.

theory_nodes.concept: Triple threat -- DJ + producer + MC in one person; rare at high level; the most complete hip-hop artist in the Seattle ecosystem
examples: DJ: turntablist, live performance, vinyl culture.
Producer: beat-making, studio engineering, others' records.
MC: rapping, lyricism, solo artistry, feature verses.
Few figures operate at high level in all three.
theory_nodes.description: Triple threat. DJ + Producer + MC. Rare completeness.

genealogy.relationship: Vitamin D at 147. Triple threat documented.

theory_nodes.concept: Central District documentary -- geographically specific lyricism; street names, landmarks, characters; music as neighborhood archive; documenting the CD at a time of rapid change
examples: Lyrics reference specific CD streets, businesses, people.
Documentary quality: recording a place at a specific time.
The CD undergoing gentrification during Vitamin D's career.
Conception Records as physical and cultural CD anchor.
theory_nodes.description: Central District documentary. Music as neighborhood archive.

genealogy.relationship: Vitamin D MC at 147. CD documentary.

cross_references.relationship: RETURN! Dual-facet pattern. DJ/producer at 138, MC/artist at 147. 9-degree gap. Triple threat complete.
PREVIOUS DEGREE! Genre-dissolving duo (146) to triple-threat solo MC (147). Both Central District. Both part of the scene.
PARALLEL PATTERN! Sabzi multi-entry (5 entries, 99-145). Vitamin D dual-entry (138, 147). Both document different facets of multifaceted artists.
CENTRAL DISTRICT! CD hip-hop thread: Riz (136), Vitamin D DJ (138), now Vitamin D MC (147). The neighborhood deepens.
